mnemonics: Naming Packages Like A URL Reversed (com.company.package)
Packages Are Like Folders For Your Classes
Import Statements Are Shortcuts To Avoid Writing Full Names
keyPoints: Packages help avoid name conflicts by providing a unique namespace
The 'import' keyword is used to bring classes from other packages into the current file
Package naming convention uses reverse domain name (e.g., com.example.mypackage)
commonMistakes: Forgetting to declare the package statement at the top of the file
Using the wrong case in package or import statements (Java is case-sensitive)
Not understanding the difference between importing a specific class versus using a wildcard (*)
